steps.title: Identify the grammar on each side of the blank
steps.content: Left of the blank is the comparative clause earlier than was previously supposed. Right of the blank begins the noun phrase a finding that, if true, would overturn …

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for appositives
steps.content:
A comma is needed to set off an appositive noun phrase that renames the preceding clause, so choose supposed, a (choice D).

The other choices either use a semicolon or period that requires an independent clause after it, or omit punctuation and create a fused clause.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
